name: In Vivo High-Resolution Inner Ear Imaging Grant Application

sections.name: specific_aims
sections.title: Specific Aims
sections.instructions: State the specific objectives of the proposed research, including the hypotheses to be tested. Define the key goals of the project, focusing on the development or improvement of in vivo high-resolution imaging technologies for the inner ear. Include the type of imaging that will be improved, the target resolution increase, and the potential for clinical translation.

sections.name: significance
sections.title: Significance
sections.instructions: Explain the importance of the proposed research and its potential impact on the field. Address the current limitations in inner ear imaging and how the proposed work will fill this gap. Describe the potential benefits for diagnosing and treating inner ear disorders, linking the technology's advancement to improved health outcomes and clinical practice. Justify the proposed research with the scientific background, literature and preliminary data. Explain the value of the technology development to hearing and balance disorders.

sections.name: innovation
sections.title: Innovation
sections.instructions: Describe the innovative aspects of the proposed imaging technology or techniques. Explain how the proposed technology introduces new concepts, methods or technologies, or how existing ones are used in novel ways to improve inner ear imaging. Detail the expected advantages of the proposed approach over current methods, emphasizing its unique contributions and the state-of-the-art nature of the advances.

sections.name: approach
sections.title: Approach
sections.instructions: Detail the research design and methods to achieve the project's specific aims. Describe the experimental procedures, data collection, and analysis plans. Address potential challenges and alternative strategies. Include a plan to translate the developed imaging technologies to clinical settings, including timelines and requirements for human use. Explain any limitations and solutions for translation into the clinical setting. Describe how the increase in resolution will be assessed, and how the new capabilities could change current treatment options.

sections.name: resource_sharing_plan
sections.title: Resource Sharing Plan
sections.instructions: Describe how the data and resources generated from this project will be shared. Include plans for making data, software, and other materials accessible to the broader research community. The resource sharing plan should address how the imaging data and associated resources will be made available for use by other researchers in the field.

sections.name: data_management_and_sharing_plan
sections.title: Data Management and Sharing Plan
sections.instructions: Describe how the scientific data generated from this project will be managed and shared, consistent with NIH guidelines. Include data types, standards, preservation and access approaches, and any relevant ethical considerations. The plan should address the full data lifecycle and meet the requirements described in the NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy.
keywords: NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy

sections.name: human_subjects_and_clinical_trials
sections.title: PHS Human Subjects and Clinical Trials Information
sections.instructions: Describe how human subjects will be involved in this research including plans for inclusion, protections, and data and safety monitoring. If clinical trials are included, fully detail how the proposed low-risk study adheres to the requirements of this NOFO. Specifically describe the inclusion of women and minorities, outlining plans to analyze differences across sex and gender subgroups. If human subject research is not involved, state so clearly.
